authority: Repository documentation, local policy, local skill validation, local verification, and Git-index inspection only. No provider, deployment, workflow setting, credential, message, approval, readback, or mutation authority.

finding.invariant: The active route contains only genuinely current implementation intent.
finding.target: docs/exec-plans/active/harness-governance-documentation.md and the matching current SPEC/index routes
finding.cause: All repository-local harness work was accepted, but the plan and SPEC stayed current solely to retain HGI-309's separately deferred external-authority boundary.
finding.repair: Move the accepted plan to completed history, classify the retained SPEC as implemented/supporting, update indexes and current routers, and preserve HGI-309's deferred provider boundary in retained evidence.
finding.lastProvedPostcondition: HGI-307 accepted the local repository harness closeout while explicitly retaining HGI-309 as deferred and making no external claim.

impactLedger.postcondition: Only the unrelated Codex Terra work remains active; accepted harness provenance is completed history.

impactLedger.surface: Documentation router, audit, effectiveness, and epoch provenance
impactLedger.postcondition: Current routes distinguish accepted local provenance from active intent and retain a bounded correction receipt.
The current-owner policy and test no longer require accepted harness provenance at an active path.

impactLedger.surface: READMEs, architecture, runbooks, authority, provider state, skills, lint, config, CI, and automation

A named owner must still approve one exact operation with target, environment, duration, rollback, and current readback before any external qualification.

limitations: This candidate cannot accept HGI-309 or establish provider, workflow, deployment, credential, delivery, rollback, recovery, or Production actuality.
Local checks prove repository consistency only; accepted prior evidence and deferred ownership remain addressable rather than rewritten.
nonClaims: No external system was read or changed.
The lifecycle correction does not requalify the worker or create an effectiveness claim.
rollback: Revert the lifecycle-route, documentation-policy test, and bounded correction evidence together; preserve the accepted HGI-300 through HGI-310 evidence and HGI-309 deferral.
